Title: Folder tabs and the source filter
Summary: Folders are speaker scopes (Eddie, Krang, Welcome). The source filter slices by NPC vs Terminal.
Body:
## Folder tabs

Each folder maps to a directory under the Godot project (`.../dialogs/<Speaker>/`) and shows up as a tab at the top of the [graph view](/dialogs). Click a tab to switch.

Folders are auto discovered at server boot: drop a new `characters/npcs/<robot>/dialogs/<NewSpeaker>/` folder under the Godot project and at next boot Bleepforge will surface it as a fresh tab. No code changes required.

## Per folder dots

Each tab carries small colored squares: orange when the folder contains NPC dialogs, green when it contains Terminal dialogs, both when mixed. This lets you spot at a glance which folders are which type without opening them.

## The source filter

Top right of the graph is a three way segmented control: All, NPC, Terminal. It hides sequences that do not match the active filter, plus edges with hidden endpoints. Folders that have no matches in the active filter are themselves hidden from the tab strip; if your active folder gets hidden by a filter change, the graph hops to the first visible folder automatically.

The filter persists across the graph and list views, and across tabs. It lives in localStorage.

> tip: the filter NPC and Terminal buttons keep their color even at rest. Active state shows three layered cues (inset ring, brighter palette, 2 px shadow) so you do not need color alone to see what is selected.
